evals.prompt: brainstorm 5 new features for wakey. i want it to feel really alive when i'm coding late at night
evals.expected_output: Should generate 5 companion-like features with mini-proposals, all passing the alive test, focused on late-night coding scenarios

evals.prompt: i think wakey should track how much time i spend on each app and show me a weekly report with charts and graphs. also it should integrate with toggl and clockify for time tracking
evals.expected_output: Should catch this as tool-creep/manager behavior, fail the Friend Gate, and redirect toward a more companion-like approach to time awareness

evals.prompt: we should add a microservice architecture for wakey with a separate API gateway, message queue, and database service so it can scale to handle multiple users on a network
evals.expected_output: Should catch this as massive over-engineering and scope explosion, redirect to single-binary Rust philosophy, remind that Wakey runs on ONE laptop for ONE person
